title: Xtra Crisp Mexican Ground Beef Fajitas
description: A winey, crisp, and cozy Mexican dish featuring ground beef and served with toast, this recipe is a satvik-friendly version of traditional fajitas, originating from the Chiapan region.
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the diced onion and cook until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the diced bell pepper and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Add the ground beef to the skillet, breaking it up with a spoon as it cooks.
Once the ground beef is browned, add the fajita seasoning and stir to combine.
Add the wine to the skillet and bring to a simmer, cooking until the liquid has almost completely reduced.
Reduce heat to medium-low and continue to cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Toast the bread slices until crispy and golden brown.
Serve the ground beef mixture over the toasted bread, garnished with salt and pepper to taste.
Cook ground beef to an internal temperature of 71°C / 160°F (no longer pink in the middle) before serving.
